title: OptionIterator: OptionNumberOverflow still allows first 60000 options to parse
description: In rust/lichen-coap/src/codec.rs OptionIterator, the new OptionNumberOverflow error correctly rejects cumulative delta > u16::MAX. However, the test shows that the first option (delta=60000) parses successfully before the second overflows. This means a malicious packet can still deliver a valid option with an extremely high option number (60000) before the overflow is detected. While not a security issue per se (60000 is a valid CoAP option number), the behavior may be surprising - callers should be aware that some options may be valid before an overflow error.
